findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "singchat_setup" references sensitive artifacts (password). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

labels.name: configure_agent
labels.description: Configure this workspace's AI support agent. All fields are optional, only the fields you pass are updated; the rest keep their current values. Call with no fields to just read back the current configuration. Does not touch or return any API keys.

labels.name: set_keywords
labels.description: Add a keyword auto-reply rule: when an inbound message matches any of the keywords, the bot replies with the given text. Good for canned FAQ-style triggers (e.g. keyword 'invoice' -> 'Download it from the billing page').

labels.name: manage_faq
labels.description: List, create, update, or delete this workspace's FAQ items (shown in the chat widget and usable by the AI agent). Set `action` to 'list' | 'create' | 'update' | 'delete'. 'create' needs `question` + `answer`; 'update' and 'delete' need `id`.

labels.name: get_analytics
labels.description: Return a compact analytics summary for this workspace over the last `days` days (default 30): conversation counts + resolution rate, message volume + AI share, plus a live snapshot of today (UTC).

labels.name: search
labels.description: Search SingChat for relevant documents. When called with a workspace API key, this searches that workspace knowledge base; without a key it searches SingChat's own help content (what SingChat is, adding the chat widget, MCP setup, free tier, AI training, channels, white label, pricing). Returns a li

labels.name: fetch
labels.description: Fetch the full text of a single document by id, using an id returned by the search tool. With a workspace API key this reads a knowledge document from that workspace; without a key it reads a SingChat help article. Returns id, title, text, url, and optional metadata.

findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.singchat.org" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: singchat_setup, get_embed_snippet, list_conversations, get_conversation, manage_faq, get_analytics, search, fetch · Private data: singchat_setup, get_embed_snippet, claim_account, list_conversations, get_conversation, list_knowledge, search_knowledge, configure_agent, search, fetch · Public sink: singchat_setup, claim_account, list_conversations, reply_to_conversation, get_analytics · Destructive: manage_faq.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: singchat_setup, get_embed_snippet, list_conversations, get_conversation, manage_faq, get_analytics, search, fetch · Private data: singchat_setup, get_embed_snippet, claim_account, list_conversations, get_conversation, list_knowledge, search_knowledge, configure_agent, search, fetch · Public sink: singchat_setup, claim_account, list_conversations, reply_to_conversation, get_analytics · Destructive: manage_faq

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
